summary: Russian forces are using FPV drones to destroy Ukrainian unmanned boats near Tendrov Spit and the Dnipro River mouth, while the "Rubicon" Center reports 132 targets destroyed on March 13 and 136 on March 14, including personnel and fixed-wing UAVs. Ukrainian forces are employing AI-powered HX-2 drones from Helsing, with over 6,000 delivered, and are offering partners access to combat data for AI training. Russian troops are countering with the "Yolka" anti-drone system, which has successfully intercepted Ukrainian fixed-wing "Hornet" drones. On the ground, Ukrainian FPV pilots are using tactics like throwing smoke grenades and blinding targets, while Russian forces report destroying over 20,000 enemy targets, with 35.4% being unmanned systems.
